title: NCQA_HPA_CredentialingAndRecredentialing
description: Schema for extracting structured data from NCQA HPA Standards: Credentialing and Recredentialing (CR) document. Effective for surveys on or after July 1, 2026.

document_metadata.description: Top-level metadata about the standards document itself.

title.description: Full title of the standards document.

effective_date.description: Date from which these standards are effective (YYYY-MM-DD).

standard_set.description: Name of the standard set, e.g. '2026 HPA Standards and Guidelines'.

credentialing_standards.description: List of all CR standards (CR 1 through CR 9) extracted from the document.

items.description: A single top-level CR standard (e.g. CR 1: Credentialing Policies).

standard_title.description: Full title of the standard, e.g. 'Credentialing Policies'.

intent.description: The stated intent or purpose of this standard.

elements.description: List of elements (sub-sections) within this standard (e.g. Element A, Element B).

items.description: A single element within a CR standard.

element_title.description: Full title of the element, e.g. 'Practitioner Credentialing Guidelines'.

is_must_pass.description: Whether this element is designated as a MUST-PASS element.

scoring.description: Scoring thresholds for Met, Partially Met, Not Met.

met.description: Criteria to score Met, e.g. 'The organization meets 11-13 factors'.

data_source.description: The type of evidence NCQA reviews, e.g. 'Documented process', 'Records or files'.

scope_of_review.description: Which product lines and survey types this element applies to.

look_back_periods.description: Look-back period rules for different survey types.

survey_type.description: Survey type, e.g. 'Interim Surveys', 'First Surveys', 'Renewal Surveys', 'All Surveys'.

period.description: Look-back period description, e.g. '24 months', '6 months', 'Prior to the survey date'.

exceptions.description: Any factor-level exceptions to the standard look-back period, e.g. '12 months for factors 6, 9, 13'.
Any stated exceptions or NA conditions for this element.

factors.description: Individual factors (numbered requirements) within this element.

items.description: A single factor within an element.

factor_number.description: The numeric identifier of this factor (e.g. 1, 2, 3).

factor_title.description: Short descriptive title of the factor, e.g. 'Types of practitioners'.

description.description: Full explanation of what the factor requires.

is_critical_factor.description: Whether this is a critical factor (scoring cannot exceed Partially Met if scored No).

verification_time_limit_days.description: Verification time limit in calendar days, if applicable (e.g. 120 for 120-day limit). Null if not applicable.

verification_time_limit_note.description: Any transitional note about when the time limit applies, e.g. files processed on or after July 1, 2025.

acceptable_sources.description: Acceptable verification or data sources listed for this factor.

practitioner_types_in_scope.description: Practitioner types explicitly listed as being in scope for this factor (if applicable).

is_na_for_recredentialing.description: Whether this factor is explicitly NA for recredentialing files.

examples.description: Any examples provided for this factor.

summary_of_changes.description: List of changes noted in the 'Summary of Changes' section for this element.

description.description: Description of the specific change made.

related_information.description: Any 'Related information' notes provided for this element.

practitioner_scope.description: Global rules defining which practitioners are within the scope of credentialing, as defined in CR 1, Element A.

inclusion_criteria.description: Criteria that must all be met for a practitioner to be in scope.

applicable_settings.description: Practice settings where the scope criteria apply (e.g. individual practice, telemedicine, locum tenens).

excluded_practitioners.description: Practitioner categories explicitly excluded from credentialing scope.

max_provisional_days.description: Maximum number of calendar days a practitioner may be held in provisional status.

required_verifications.description: Items that must be verified within time limits for provisional credentialing.

restrictions.description: Restrictions that apply to provisionally credentialed practitioners.

recredentialing_cycle.description: Global recredentialing cycle rules extracted from CR 4.

max_cycle_months.description: Maximum recredentialing cycle length in months (e.g. 36).

cycle_extension_conditions.description: Conditions under which the recredentialing cycle may be extended beyond the maximum.

post_extension_deadline_days.description: Number of calendar days within which the practitioner must be recredentialed after returning from an extension condition.

termination_and_reinstatement_rules.description: Rules for termination and reinstatement affecting credentialing cycle.

reinstatement_without_initial_credential.description: Maximum days after termination within which reinstatement does not require full initial credentialing.

failure_to_recredential_grace_period_day.description: Calendar days after missing the 36-month deadline within which recredentialing may still be completed before initial credentialing is required.

ongoing_monitoring.description: Global ongoing monitoring rules extracted from CR 5.

review_frequency_days.description: Frequency in calendar days for reviewing sanction/exclusion/licensure information (e.g. 30).

monitoring_service_alert_window_days.description: Days within which a new alert from a monitoring service subscription must be reviewed (e.g. 30).

adverse_event_monitoring_rules.description: Time-based rules for adverse event monitoring frequency, including effective dates.

effective_from.description: Date from which this rule applies (YYYY-MM-DD). Null if no start bound.

frequency.description: Required monitoring frequency, e.g. 'At least monthly', 'At least every 6 months'.

complaint_review_rules.description: Rules for collecting and reviewing member complaints.

individual_complaint_action.description: Required action upon receipt of a practitioner-specific complaint.

aggregate_review_frequency_months.description: Frequency in months for evaluating the full history of complaints for all practitioners (e.g. 6).

delegation_rules.description: Global delegation rules extracted from CR 9.

agreement_required_factors.description: List of items that a written delegation agreement must address.

predelegation_evaluation_window_months.description: Number of months before delegation implementation within which predelegation evaluation must occur (e.g. 12).

annual_file_audit_methods.description: Acceptable methodologies for the annual delegate credentialing file audit.

effectiveness_audit_window.description: Time window after annual audit within which the corrective-action effectiveness audit must occur.

automatic_credit_conditions.description: Conditions under which automatic credit is available for delegation elements.

information_integrity_rules.description: Global credentialing information integrity rules extracted from CR 8.

inappropriate_documentation_types.description: Explicitly prohibited documentation and update actions.

audit_sample_methodology.description: Rules for determining the annual audit sample size.

percentage.description: Percentage of eligible files to audit (e.g. 5 for 5%).

max_files.description: Maximum number of files to audit, whichever is less than the percentage calculation (e.g. 50).

min_initial_credentialing_files.description: Minimum number of initial credentialing files in the sample (e.g. 10).

min_recredentialing_files.description: Minimum number of recredentialing files in the sample (e.g. 10).

annual_training_required_topics.description: Topics on which credentialing staff must be trained annually.

ncqa_reportable_threshold_percentage.description: Threshold percentage of noncompliant files that triggers a mandatory self-report to NCQA (e.g. 5 for 5%).
